title: FIDE
text: Home * Organizations * FIDE FIDE Logo [1] The Fédération Internationale des Échecs (FIDE) or World Chess Federation is an international organization that acts as the governing body of international chess competition, organizing the World Chess Championship , the Women's World Chess Championship , the World Junior and Girls U-20 Chess Championship , the World Senior Chess Championship [2] , the World Youth Chess Championship , the Chess Olympiad , the World Team Chess Championship and regional championships. The FIDE was founded 1924 in Paris at the 1st unofficial Chess Olympiad as a kind of players' union by 15 participants and representatives of their countries. Contents 1 Founding Members 2 FIDE Presidents 3 FIDE and ICGA 4 FIDE CC Events 5 Forum Posts 6 External Links 7 References Founding Members [ edit ] [3] [4] [5] István Abonyi Roberto Grau Ion Gudju Florenziano Marusi Marc Nicolet Jakov M. Ovadija Count Ignacio de Penalver y Zamora Major Francis Hooper Rawlins Alexander Rueb Karel Skalička Stephen Francis Smith Izaak Towbin Anatol Tschepurnoff Pierre Vincent Leon Willem Weltjens FIDE Presidents [ edit ] President from until Alexander Rueb 1924 1949 Folke Rogard 1949 1970 Max Euwe 1970 1978 Friðrik Ólafsson 1978 1982 Florencio Campomanes 1982 1995 Kirsan Ilyumzhinov 1995 2018 Arkady Dvorkovich 2018 FIDE and ICGA [ edit ] The ICGA is recognized as Affiliated International Organization of the FIDE [6] . The WCCC 2006 along with the 37th Chess Olympiad in Turin was the first and so far the only time the ICGA was “sequentially assembled” by the FIDE [7] .
